rules_or_instructions: 1.0 GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS
General Instructions
Follow the following instructions.
1. Follow the line clearance system before starting the operations as per respective SOP No.: PAP-QA-002.
2. Follow the current good manufacturing practices.
3. Follow the approved Standard Operating Procedures.
4. Ensure that Personnel are trained for the allocated job.
5. When you document, ensure that the entry is correct and legible. If any discrepancy or mistake is observed during documentation, correct as per SOP No. IR-QA-002.
6. Ensure that plant utility, RLAF, LAF and AHU units are in running condition.
7. Read the manufacturing instructions before proceeding with operations and follow it and document each operation in BMR.
8. Ensure that all required RM and PPM are approved.
9. Follow entry-exit procedure into respective area as per SOP No. PAP-SM-001 and PAP-SM-002.
10. Ensure the environmental condition %RH, Temperature and Differential pressure are within limit.
11. In case additional pages are required, get the same issued from QA and attach.
12. Perform the filter integrity test for sterilizing grade filter used in batch before sterilization and after filtration.
13. Nitrogen purging is required during complete manufacturing process and till the end of filling process (if applicable).
14. Store the bulk solution under nitrogen blanketing (if applicable).
15. Review the Audit trail of equipment's as per SOP No. IR-MF-001.
16. Before signature at specified place, follow the responsibility as per below.
17. Doer: A person who perform the activity or gets things done with efficiency according to procedure.
18. Done By: Signature of person doing the operation, test, inspection, calculation etc. being recorded.
19. Checked By: The person responsible for observing the actual procedure or witnessing performance of the task or performing an independent check to ensure the task was done correctly.
20. Verified By: A person who ensure or demonstrates that activity performed by doer is true, accurate or justified.
21. Reviewed By: Signature of the person responsible for reviewing and examining the documentation certifying that the record/protocol/report has been reviewed and found completed and in compliance.
22. Ensured by: The person responsible for ensuring the procedure or witnessing performance of the task or performing an independent check to ensure the task was done correctly.

rules_or_instructions: 23. Paste the signed and verified printout and issuance label on Exhibit VIII (Page for pasting labels/ Printout) as per SOP No.: PAP-SM-054 and attach with BMR.

24. Note: Perform the Printing on flip off seal quality/defects checks as per SOP No. PAP-SM-061 and record the details in Form No. PAP-SM-061-F008 of SOP No. PAP-SM-061 and attach the record with BMR
Product Specific Instruction:
1. Dibasic Potassium Phosphate, USP is deliquescent in nature. Dispense the API at NMT 30% RH and in minimum exposure time or in NMT 2 hours to prevent the moisture absorption.
2. Dispense the Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP-NF and Dibasic Potassium Phosphate, USP in polybag/suitable container with suitable capacity and pack tightly to avoid undue exposure of moisture.

3. After completion of dispensing process, store remaining quantity of Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP-NF and Dibasic Potassium Phosphate, USP tightly with nitrogen purging (0.2 to 1.0 bar), in its vendor supplied container, at controlled room temperature with allowable excursion between 15.0°C and 30.0°C.
4. Use 0.2 µ filtered Nitrogen only to apply pressure for filtration/transfer of bulk solution.
5. The maximum pressure for first and final filtration of solution should be NMT 36.2 psi or 2.5 bars.
6. Bulk solution should be released for filtration and filling without the results of In-process tests. However, all in-process test data will be a part of batch release decision.
7. Store the finished product at 20.0°C to 25.0°C (68° F to 77° F).
8. The total maximum contact time of product with filter and tube should not be more than 24 hrs.
9. Unfiltered bulk solution hold time in SS 316L vessel is upto 24 hrs. at 25.0°C ± 5.0°C.
10. Filtered bulk solution hold time in SS 316L vessel is upto 24 hrs. at 25.0°C ± 5.0°C.
11. The tubing contact time should not exceed 24hr at 25.0°C ± 5.0°C.
12. Consider the First filtration is clarification filtration through 1.2 µm filter.

table_json.NAME OF PRODUCT: Potassium Phosphates Injection USP, Phosphorus 3 mmol/mL and Potassium 4.4 mEq/mL (15 mL)

rules_or_instructions: MASTER FORMULA
Master formula (Theoretical Qty Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP- NF) ^ Reference to master formula
Theoretical Qty. of Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP- NF [TQE](g)
Qty./mL × batch size in mL
Master formula (Theoretical Qty Dibasic Potassium Phosphate USP)
Theoretical Qty. of Dibasic Potassium Phosphate USP [TQE](g)
Qty./mL × batch size in mL
This quantity is calculated based on 100% w/w assay on dried basis of Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP-NF.
Actual quantity to be added is calculated as (mg)
224 × 100 / Assay on dried basis × 100 / (100 - (Loss on Drying))
Note: If Assay of Monobasic Potassium Phosphate is >100%, take 100% for calculation.
This quantity is calculated based on 100% w/w assay on dried basis of Dibasic Potassium Phosphate USP
Actual quantity to be added is calculated as (mg)
236 × 100 / Assay on dried basis × 100 / (100 - Loss on Drying)
Note: If Assay of Dibasic Potassium Phosphate is >100%, take 100% for calculation.

markdown_page:
amneal BATCH MANUFACTURING RECORD
NAME OF PRODUCT Potassium Phosphates Injection USP, Phosphorus 3 mmol/mL and Potassium 4.4 mEq/mL (15 mL) BATCH NO.
BMN NO. BMN-PA-040-10
BATCH SIZE 500.0 L/658.0 Kg/31250 Vials AH250014
PAGE NO. Page 16 of 170
7.0 MASTER FORMULA

Master formula (Theoretical Qty Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP- NF) ^ Reference to master formula

Theoretical Qty. of Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP- NF [TQE](g)
= \text{Qty./mL} \times \text{batch size in mL}
= 224 \text{ mg/mL} \times 500000 \text{ mL}
= 112000000 \text{ mg} \approx 112000 \text{ g} \approx 112.0 \text{ Kg}

Master formula (Theoretical Qty Dibasic Potassium Phosphate USP)

Theoretical Qty. of Dibasic Potassium Phosphate USP [TQE](g)
= \text{Qty./mL} \times \text{batch size in mL}
= 236 \text{ mg/mL} \times 500000 \text{ mL}
= 118000000 \text{ mg} \approx 118000 \text{ g} \approx 118.0 \text{ Kg}

This quantity is calculated based on 100% w/w assay on dried basis of Monobasic Potassium Phosphate, USP-NF.
Actual quantity to be added is calculated as (mg)

\frac{224 \times 100}{\text{Assay on dried basis}} \times \frac{100}{100 - (\text{Loss on Drying})}

Note: If Assay of Monobasic Potassium Phosphate is >100%, take 100% for calculation.

This quantity is calculated based on 100% w/w assay on dried basis of Dibasic Potassium Phosphate USP
Actual quantity to be added is calculated as (mg)

\frac{236 \times 100}{\text{Assay on dried basis}} \times \frac{100}{100 - \text{Loss on Drying}}

Note: If Assay of Dibasic Potassium Phosphate is >100%, take 100% for calculation.

rules_or_instructions: 15.0 CLEAN IN PLACE (CIP) & STERILIZATION IN PLACE (SIP) DETAILS
Follow the schematic flow as below for CIP/cleaning & SIP as below:
Loop 1: Mixing Vessel (100L, EQP/P1/PRD/067) → Product piping → First filter (1.2 µ) * housing and Diaphragm valve → Product piping
Loop 2: Mixing Vessel (800L, EQP/P1/PRD/068) → Product piping → First filter (1.2 µ) * housing and Diaphragm valve → Product piping
Loop 3: Filtration Vessel (800L, EQP/P1/PRD/069) → Product Piping and Diaphragm valve
Loop 4: Final filter (0.2 µ)# / Empty filter housing** and Diaphragm valve → Product piping → Vial Filling Machine (EQP/P1/PRD/072)
Loop 5: Block Valve → Product Piping → Diaphragm Valves
*Remove the filter before CIP start.
# Remove the filter before CIP start if cartridge filter is used.
** Empty filter housing to be used for SIP in case if capsule filter is used.

↓

Loop 5: Block Valve → Product Piping → Diaphragm Valves

*Remove the filter before CIP start.

# Remove the filter before CIP start if cartridge filter is used.

** Empty filter housing to be used for SIP in case if capsule filter is used.
